messages.subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] ODE question
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

I have a system of ODEs for an infectious disease model. It is a heterogeneous SEIR model, where each S, E, I, R type compartment has n strata. Depending on the situation, n is typically of the order of 10. My question is can I define the system of ODEs by some kind of vectorization and pass to deSolve to solve? Rather than type out the 10*4=40 differential equations, I would prefer to set it up as 4 vectorized diff eqs for each S, E, I, R. How do I do this in R and specify 4 initial conditions consisting of vectors of length 10 then solve it with deSolve?

I hope my question makes sense and thanks in advance.

Andrew

messages.subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] ODE question
messages.body_plain:
Andrew,
 
I am not sure, but perhaps this is what you want?
 

SIR <- function(t, y, p) {
  S <- y[1:100]
  I <- y[101:200]  
  R <- y[201:300]
 
  dS<- -0.1*I*S
  dI <- 0.1*I*S - 0.15*I  
  dR <- 0.15*I
 
list(c(dS, dI, dR))
}
 
yini <- c(S=1:100, I = 2:101, R = rep(0,100))
out <- ode(y= yini, times=0:1000, p=0, func=SIR)

out2<- ode.1D(y= yini, times=0:1000, p=0, func=SIR, nspec=3)
 
 
The last solution method (ode.1D) is faster only if you have lots of "strata".
 

Karline
________________________________

From: r-sig-dynamic-models-bounces at r-project.org on behalf of Hill, Andrew N.
Sent: Fri 7/2/2010 6:25 AM
To: r-sig-dynamic-models at r-project.org

I have a system of ODEs for an infectious disease model. It is a heterogeneous SEIR model, where each S, E, I, R type compartment has n strata. Depending on the situation, n is typically of the order of 10. My question is can I define the system of ODEs by some kind of vectorization and pass to deSolve to solve? Rather than type out the 10*4=40 differential equations, I would prefer to set it up as 4 vectorized diff eqs for each S, E, I, R. How do I do this in R and specify 4 initial conditions consisting of vectors of length 10 then solve it with deSolve?

I hope my question makes sense and thanks in advance.

Andrew

messages.subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] ODE question
messages.body_plain:
On 02.07.2010 06:25, Hill, Andrew N. wrote:
> Hello,
>
> I have a system of ODEs for an infectious disease model. It is a
> heterogeneous SEIR model, where each S, E, I, R type compartment has
> n strata. Depending on the situation, n is typically of the order of
> 10. My question is can I define the system of ODEs by some kind of
> vectorization and pass to deSolve to solve? Rather than type out the
> 10*4=40 differential equations, I would prefer to set it up as 4
> vectorized diff eqs for each S, E, I, R. How do I do this in R and
> specify 4 initial conditions consisting of vectors of length 10 then
> solve it with deSolve?
>
> I hope my question makes sense and thanks in advance.
>
> Andrew

Yes, of course, it makes sense!

Do you mean something like this:

##------------------------------------------------------------------------------
library(deSolve)

func <- function(t, y, p) {
    S <- y[1:2]
    I <- y[3:4]
    R <- y[5:6]
    dS <-  - p$beta * S * I
    dI <-    p$beta * S *I - p$nu * I
    dR <-    p$nu * I
    list(c(dS, dI, dR))
}

## note that parms can also be alist
parms <- list(beta=c(0.1, 0.2), nu=c(0.1, 0.1))
init <- c(S1 = 10, S2 = 8, I1 = 1, I2 = 2, R1 = 0, R2 = 0)
times <- seq(1, 10, 0.1)

out <- ode(init, times, func, parms)

plot(out)
##------------------------------------------------------------------------------


See also:

https://stat.ethz.ch/pipermail/r-help/2009-June/201212.html

for another example that uses an interaction matrix.

Hope it helps

Thomas Petzoldt
